mission: Reduce the live support-side Bogoliubov pairing frontier to its minimal scalar production history on the lowest-shell J=1/2 sector.

theorem_1_irrep_pairing_uniqueness.statement: On a single J=1/2 irreducible doublet, every SO(3)-equivariant antisymmetric fermion pairing form is proportional to the canonical epsilon tensor. Therefore the lowest-shell singlet pair-creation operator is unique up to scalar.

conditions: restrict to the admitted lowest-shell J=1/2 source sector
require uniformity over the indistinguishable active puncture copies in the collective support channel
theorem_2_collective_scalar_reduction.statement: Under the stated symmetry conditions, the support-side pairing interaction reduces from a general kernel Delta to one scalar production history lambda(R) multiplying the unique collective singlet pair operator.

theorem_3_uniform_activation_target.status: derived / exact target reduction on the scalar route
theorem_3_uniform_activation_target.statement: If the scalar pairing route produces a uniform mean occupation fraction over the available J=1/2 modes at the source crossing radius, then the exact required occupation fraction is f_req = N_required/N_total and the corresponding Bogoliubov angle satisfies sin^2(theta_req)=f_req.

final_boundary.safe_statement: The live support-side Paper 35 frontier is no longer a general operator kernel. On the symmetry-reduced lowest-shell collective channel it collapses to one scalar production history lambda(R), equivalently one scalar Bogoliubov-angle history theta(R).
final_boundary.remaining_object: a theorem deriving the scalar production history lambda(R) or theta(R) on the support branch
